title: Missing `quay.io/skopeo/stable:v1.11.0` image
body:
I see that [v11.0.0](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/releases/tag/v1.11.0) has been released, but the image is [missing](https://quay.io/repository/skopeo/stable?tab=tags) from quay.io.

The latest image available is v1.10.0, which was updated on quay.io about 2 hours ago.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report. Per https://github.com/containers/skopeo/tree/main/contrib/skopeoimage , the stable repo contains Fedora packages that are considered stable by Fedora.

That Fedora process is ongoing: https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/?packages=skopeo 

Cc: @lsm5
@mattclay it's gonna take a little longer, I just found out another issue that was causing gating test failures. Once I have the new builds, I'll update here. please add karma.
Please add karma to https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2023-11be4d0146 so it can move to stable faster (and get pushed to quay).
I see the `stable:v1.11` image exists now, but there's no `stable:v1.11.0` -- is that to be expected?

Oops.

So what seems to have happened here is that the `v1.11.0` tag is placed slightly incorrectly, and points at a version that identifies itself, in `skopeo --version`, as `v11.1.1-dev`; and that’s the image tag that now exists on `quay.io`.

---

One question: what to do about that incorrect tag.
- We probably could move the `v1.11.0` tag in the git repo; I think that’s mostly a bad idea.
- We could change the Fedora packaging to use the right commit, and report the right version, but it would not match the upstream tag. That also seems surprising and risky.
- Practically speaking, I think this is going to be much less of a concern with the upcoming `v1.11.1` release. Assuming we make that one correctly.

So I think leaving `v1.11.0` be and waiting for the next release seems easiest.

---

Another question: is there a reasonable way to prevent such a mistake?
- Some kind of Git (GitHub?) hook to reject tags with mismatching versions?
- A `make` target to create the version bump commits and tags correctly?

Arguably, _one_ instance of a process mistake after so many tags (79 now) might not be a good reason to worry about this.

But then, we have a very similar versioning process across those repos, so automating this a bit more for all those projects might pay off.

Cc: @rhatdan @cevich

There's no check/control possible once the tag is pushed - unlesss the tag-pushing itself is done via a script, then (obviously) having some checks is possible.

A `make` target would be sexy, but how would it know what kind of release you're doing.  I think you'd need at least three targets, `major`, `minor`, and `patch`.  Then you'd need some way to translate those targets into updates in a file - the real tricky part.

IMHO, I think this can be called a "woopsie" and we move on.  I can delete the image on quay, and as long as the code is fixed, it should not re-appear.  Is this okay?

(Something like `make release new-tag='1.20'` might be possible, or just maintain a shell script.)

> IMHO, I think this can be called a "woopsie" and we move on. 

I’m fine with that.

> I can delete the image on quay,

I’d somewhat prefer waiting until we actually have a `1.11.1`, so that the structure of the repo (individually-tagged images + latest-x.y tag) is preserved.
Okay.  How should we remember that, is having this issue open enough?
I think that, + a Gmail sleep feature in my inbox, could work.
Created  https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2023-33cfd04d6c . Will take about a week to land in the stable repos. Please add karma if possible.

Following up: I searched both skopeo registries on quay but found nothing tagged `v11*`.  With the actual `v1.11.1` release, the `latest` and tagged images will fix themselves as soon as packages go out to `updates` and `updates-testing`.
@cevich I see an `v1.11.1-dev` on https://quay.io/repository/skopeo/stable?tab=tags . (Sure, that’s not ideal because the tag doesn’t have the expected form; still, you said “nothing tagged”.)

Oh! Good catch I missed that one.  I'll delete it.

Edit/context: If it's left there, other similar looking tags will be udpated but this one will be 'left behind' possibly confusing users.  It's better to pretend it never existed.
OTOH removing a tag might break existing deployments… and won’t the automation recreate it (in one of the repos, at least) within 24 hours anyway, until 1.11.1 is actually in Fedora stable?
It's too late, I already removed it.  Yes once it hits 'updates' a `v1.11.1` tagged image will show up.  I think it's not worth worrying about (unless the image doesn't appear).

Circling back, there is now a `v1`, `v1.11`, `v1.11.1` and `latest`, all pointing at the same image.

(There is, also, a `v1.11.1-dev` tag, created after the previous manual removal.)
